commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the LINE confirm directive parser to split `label:data` at the first colon, preserve bare URL casing, and add regression tests for labeled and bare URL confirm action data.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +28. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. as a high-confidence source-level reproduction. Current main and v2026.6.10 split confirm action segments with `split(":")`, which truncates labeled URL action data at the scheme colon.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair remains; the blocker is contributor real-behavior proof or maintainer proof override.

target.title: fix(line): preserve URL action data in confirm directives past the scheme colon
target.body:
## Summary

The `[[confirm: ...]]` directive parser in `extensions/line/src/reply-payload-transform.ts` split each `label:data` action segment with `yesPart.split(":")`. When the data is a URL (e.g. `Open:https://example.com/path?q=1`), `split(":")` breaks on the scheme colon too, so the destructured `[label, data]` kept only `["Open", "https"]` — `confirmData`/`cancelData` was truncated to `"https"`, dropping the real URL. The sibling `[[buttons: ...]]` parser already splits URL-aware (first colon + `http(s)://` guard); confirm did not.

### Changes
- `reply-payload-transform.ts` — parse confirm `label:data` with a first-colon split that treats a bare URL (or no-colon segment) as having no separator, preserving the full URL data. Default yes/no and `label:data` (e.g. `OK:action=confirm`) behavior is unchanged.
- `reply-payload-transform.test.ts` — added a confirm case with URL action data.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a confirm directive whose action data is a URL had that URL truncated at `https`. After the fix the full URL is preserved.

Real environment tested: Node 22.22.1, macOS, no network/model. Vitest exercises the real exported `parseLineDirectives`. BEFORE is `origin/main`'s file (swapped via `git show`).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/line/src/reply-payload-transform.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
input: "[[confirm: Open this? | Open:https://example.com/path?q=1 | Cancel]]"
BEFORE: confirmLabel="Open"  confirmData="https"                          (URL truncated)
AFTER:  confirmLabel="Open"  confirmData="https://example.com/path?q=1"   (URL preserved)
existing: "OK:action=confirm" -> "action=confirm", "Yes" -> "yes"          (unchanged)
```

Observed result after fix: confirm URL data survives; the existing label:data and default yes/no cases are unchanged. The new test fails on `origin/main` and passes after.

What was not tested: a live LINE template send (the test drives the real `parseLineDirectives`). The full existing suite stays green.

## Additional checks
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/line/src/reply-payload-transform.test.ts` passes; fail-before verified by swapping in `origin/main`'s file. Extension `oxlint` clean.
target.author: ly-wang19

commands.command: re-review — addressed the rank-ups: (1) the bare-url branch no longer lowercases uri data (paths/queries are case-sensitive) — only non-url no-separator segments keep the lowercased-postback behavior; added a bare-url regression test that fails on the prior commit. proof note: `parselinedirectives` is a pure synchronous function over a plain object; the colocated vitest drives the exact production function the line send path calls (`buildtemplatemessagefrompayload` consumes its `confirmdata`), so there's no live-channel surface to capture beyond it — requesting a maintainer proof override for this pure-helper path if the unit proof is insufficient
commands.freeform_prompt: — addressed the rank-ups: (1) the bare-URL branch no longer lowercases URI data (paths/queries are case-sensitive) — only non-URL no-separator segments keep the lowercased-postback behavior; added a bare-URL regression test that fails on the prior commit. Proof note: `parseLineDirectives` is a pure synchronous function over a plain object; the colocated Vitest drives the exact production function the LINE send path calls (`buildTemplateMessageFromPayload` consumes its `confirmData`), so there's no live-channel surface to capture beyond it — requesting a maintainer proof override for this pure-helper path if the unit proof is insufficient.
